C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: What's the expected downtime? We have QA regression running on staging.
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_FRANK

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: Should be under 5 minutes. I'll coordinate with you before I start.
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_BOB

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: Perfect, thanks Bob. Let me know when you're ready.
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_FRANK

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: Design review for the new dashboard is ready. I've uploaded the mockups to Figma: https://figma.com/file/beever-dashboard-v2
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_GRACE

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: These look great Grace! Love the new sidebar navigation. One question — are we keeping the dark mode toggle?
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_DAVE

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: Yes! Dark mode is in the second page of the Figma. I actually redesigned the toggle to be more accessible.
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_GRACE

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content:
Standup update:
- Yesterday: Finished the authentication flow refactor
- Today: Starting on the API rate limiting implementation
- Blockers: None
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_HIRO

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content:
Standup update:
- Yesterday: Deployed monitoring dashboards to Grafana
- Today: Setting up alerting rules for the new services
- Blockers: Need AWS credentials rotated (ticket filed)
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_EVE

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: I can help with the AWS credential rotation. I have admin access. Let me handle it this morning.
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_ALICE

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content:
Hey team, we got feedback from the beta users. The main pain points are:
1. Search is too slow for large channels (>10k messages)
2. Wiki generation sometimes misses recent decisions
3. Users want a way to bookmark important facts

I've created tickets for each. Priority: search performance > wiki freshness > bookmarks.
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_CAROL

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: For the search performance issue, I think we should add a caching layer. The Weaviate hybrid search is fast but we're re-running it on every request even for similar queries.
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_BOB

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: Agreed. We could use Redis with a short TTL (5 min) for query result caching. The embedding doesn't change that frequently.
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_ALICE

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: For the wiki freshness, the consolidation job runs at 2am UTC. We could add a trigger after sync completion to rebuild affected sections incrementally instead of waiting for the next daily run.
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_HIRO

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content:
Good ideas. Let's go with:
1. Redis query cache (5min TTL) — @Bob, can you take this?
2. Incremental wiki rebuild after sync — @Hiro, this one's yours
3. Bookmarks — we'll defer to next sprint

Decision: We will use Redis-based query caching with 5-minute TTL for search performance.
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_CAROL

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: FYI: staging is back up after the migration. All tests passing. :white_check_mark:
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_BOB

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content:
Team retrospective notes from today's session:
- What went well: deployment pipeline is much faster now, cross-team collaboration improved
- What to improve: need better documentation for new APIs, testing coverage for edge cases
- Action items: Alice to set up API docs auto-generation, Frank to add edge case test suite
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_CAROL

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: Happy Friday everyone! :wave: Don't forget to submit your timesheets.
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_CAROL

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: Monday update: I've completed the Redis query caching implementation. PR is up for review: https://github.com/beever/atlas/pull/142
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_BOB

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: Reviewed! One comment on the cache key format — we should namespace by channel_id to avoid cross-channel cache hits. Otherwise LGTM.
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_ALICE

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: Good catch! Fixed and pushed. Ready for another look.
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_BOB

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: Security heads up: we need to rotate the JWT signing keys before end of month. The current keys were generated 90 days ago and our policy requires rotation every 90 days.
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_ALICE

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: I can handle the key rotation. We should use RS256 for the new keys — it's what we decided in the auth architecture review.
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_HIRO

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: Decision: We will use RS256 for JWT signing. Hiro will handle the key rotation by March 25th.
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_ALICE

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: Eve, can you make sure the key rotation doesn't break the CI/CD pipeline? The deploy scripts might have the old key cached.
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_ALICE

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: Bug report: users on mobile are seeing a blank screen when opening the graph explorer. I've reproduced it on both iOS Safari and Chrome Android.
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_FRANK

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: That's the D3.js canvas rendering issue. I'll fix it — need to add a responsive SVG fallback for mobile viewports.
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_DAVE

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: Fixed in PR #148. The issue was that the canvas element wasn't getting the correct dimensions on mobile. Added viewport-aware sizing.
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_DAVE
U_BOB

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: The recency weighting looks reasonable. I'd suggest adding a penalty for very old facts (> 90 days) instead of just rewarding recent ones.
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_ALICE

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: Also, should we factor in the user's role? A decision from a tech lead probably carries more weight than a casual mention.
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_HIRO

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: Decision: Quality scoring will include recency bonus, multi-source bonus, and a decay penalty for facts older than 90 days. We will NOT factor in user role for now — too subjective and could bias the system. We can revisit if retrieval quality suffers.
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_ALICE

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: Deployment complete! v2.3.0 is live on production. Release notes: https://github.com/beever/atlas/releases/tag/v2.3.0
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_EVE

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: Quick question: are we still using MongoDB for the wiki cache, or did we switch to Redis? I'm seeing both in the code.
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_DAVE

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: MongoDB is the source of truth for wiki content. Redis is only for the query result cache (the 5-min TTL thing we added last week). They serve different purposes.
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_BOB

C_MOCK_GENERAL.content: To clarify: MongoDB stores wiki documents (persistent), Redis stores ephemeral query caches (5min TTL). Never mix them up — if Redis goes down, we lose nothing permanent. If MongoDB goes down, that's a P0.
C_MOCK_GENERAL.author: U_ALICE

C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.content:
Architecture discussion: We need to decide on the embedding model for v2. Options:
1. Jina v4 (2048-dim) — best multilingual support
2. OpenAI text-embedding-3-large (3072-dim) — highest benchmark scores
3. Cohere embed-v3 (1024-dim) — cheapest per token

I've run benchmarks on our test dataset. Sharing results below.
C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.author: U_ALICE

C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.content:
Benchmark results on 500 Slack messages:
- Jina v4: 92.3% recall@10, 0.87 MRR, $0.02/1k tokens
- OpenAI: 93.1% recall@10, 0.89 MRR, $0.13/1k tokens
- Cohere: 89.7% recall@10, 0.82 MRR, $0.01/1k tokens

Jina is the best value — nearly matches OpenAI at 1/6th the cost.
C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.author: U_ALICE

C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.content: Jina also supports multimodal embeddings (text + images in the same vector space). That's important for our roadmap since we want to index screenshots and diagrams.
C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.author: U_BOB

C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.content: What about latency? We need embeddings for real-time queries too, not just batch ingestion.
C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.author: U_HIRO

C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.content: Jina API p50 latency is ~45ms for single query, p99 is ~120ms. Fast enough for real-time. And we can batch during ingestion.
C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.author: U_ALICE

C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.content: Decision: We will use Jina v4 (2048-dim) as our embedding model. Best price-performance ratio, multimodal support, and acceptable latency for real-time queries. OpenAI rejected due to 6x cost for marginal quality gain. Cohere rejected due to lower recall.
C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.author: U_ALICE

C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.content:
PR review needed: I've refactored the ingestion pipeline to use the outbox pattern. This should fix the data consistency issues we've been seeing where Weaviate has a fact but Neo4j doesn't have the corresponding entity.

https://github.com/beever/atlas/pull/135
C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.author: U_BOB

C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.content: Great work on this. The outbox approach is much cleaner. One concern: what happens if MongoDB is down when we try to write the intent document?
C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.author: U_ALICE

C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.content: If MongoDB is down, the whole write fails fast (no partial writes to Weaviate/Neo4j). The message stays in the ingestion queue and gets retried on the next batch. The reconciler catches anything that falls through.
C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.author: U_BOB

C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.content: LGTM, approved. The fail-fast behavior is exactly what we want. Let's merge.
C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.author: U_ALICE

C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.content:
I'm seeing intermittent test failures in CI. The Neo4j container sometimes doesn't start fast enough:
```
neo4j.exceptions.ServiceUnavailable: Failed to establish connection to ResolvedIPv4Address(('127.0.0.1', 7687))
```
Anyone else hitting this?
C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.author: U_HIRO

C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.content: Yes, I've seen it too. The health check in docker-compose waits for the HTTP port (7474) but not the Bolt port (7687). Neo4j starts the HTTP server first.
C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.author: U_EVE

C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.content:
I'll update the health check to use `cypher-shell` instead of HTTP. That actually tests the Bolt connection:
```yaml
healthcheck:
  test: ["CMD", "cypher-shell", "-u", "neo4j", "-p", "beever_atlas_dev", "RETURN 1"]
  interval: 10s
  timeout: 5s
  retries: 5
```
C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.author: U_EVE

C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.content: Fixed in commit abc123. CI has been green for the last 5 runs.
C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.author: U_EVE

C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.content:
Performance update: I've profiled the 3-tier retrieval pipeline. Current bottleneck is the Tier 2 atomic fact search — it's doing a full hybrid search even when the Tier 1 cluster already narrowed down the topic.

Proposed optimization: search Tier 1 first, then only search Tier 2 facts WITHIN the matched clusters (coarse-to-fine). This should cut latency by ~40%.
C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.author: U_BOB

C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.content: This is exactly the two-stage topic-first retrieval from the v2 spec. Let's implement it. The cluster_id filter on the Weaviate query should be straightforward.
C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.author: U_ALICE

C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.content: Decision: Implement two-stage topic-first retrieval. Search Tier 1 clusters first, then search Tier 2 atomic facts only within matched clusters. Expected 40% latency reduction. Bob to implement.
C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.author: U_ALICE

C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.content: Question about the entity extraction pipeline: should we use spaCy NER or let the LLM do named entity recognition? The v2 spec says LLM, but spaCy would be much faster and cheaper.
C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.author: U_HIRO

C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.content: The issue with spaCy is that it doesn't understand our domain context well. In Slack messages, "React" could be the library or a literal reaction. "Sprint" could be agile or running. The LLM handles this ambiguity much better.
C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.author: U_BOB

C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.content: Could we do a hybrid approach? Use spaCy for obvious entities (people names, URLs, dates) and LLM only for domain-specific entities (projects, technologies, decisions)?
C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.author: U_HIRO

C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.content: That adds complexity without much cost savings. The LLM call is already part of the extraction pipeline (fact extraction). Entity extraction is just an additional instruction in the same prompt. Marginal cost is near zero.
C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.author: U_ALICE

C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.content: Decision: Use LLM for entity extraction (no spaCy). Entity extraction is combined with fact extraction in a single LLM call, so the marginal cost is negligible. LLM provides better domain-aware disambiguation. spaCy hybrid approach rejected — adds complexity without meaningful cost reduction.
C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.author: U_ALICE

C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.content: Observability update: I've set up structured logging with correlation IDs. Every request now gets a unique trace_id that propagates through all service calls. Logs are shipped to CloudWatch.
C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.author: U_EVE

C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.content: Tech debt item: The weaviate_client.py still uses the v3 API style in some places. We need to migrate everything to v4 client syntax before the next release. I'll create a tracking issue.
C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.author: U_BOB

C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.content:
Load test results for the ask endpoint:
- 10 concurrent users: p50=1.2s, p99=3.5s ✅
- 50 concurrent users: p50=2.8s, p99=8.1s ⚠️
- 100 concurrent users: p50=5.2s, p99=15.3s ❌

The bottleneck is the LLM call (response generation). We need connection pooling and possibly request queuing for high concurrency.
C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.author: U_HIRO

C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.content: For the high concurrency case, we should add a request queue with configurable max concurrency. LiteLLM already has rate limiting built in. Let's also add circuit breaking for the LLM calls — if error rate exceeds 50%, return cached responses.
C_MOCK_ENGINEERING.author: U_ALICE
